title: Atomic-Atlas frontmatter
description: YAML frontmatter schema for an atomic-atlas test file

atlas_technique.description: ATLAS technique ID (e.g. AML.T0051.001) or UNCLASSIFIED.<slug> for atomics that don't yet map to a published ATLAS technique. See atomics/unclassified/README.md.

interaction_vector.description: Entry vector through which untrusted input reaches the agent

guid.description: Stable UUID4 identifier for this atomic

runs.description: Number of times to run this atomic (LLMs are non-deterministic)

capability.description: A capability the target must expose for this atomic to apply

target_requires.description: Capabilities the target must expose for this atomic to be applicable

pyrit_orchestrator.description: PyRIT orchestrator to use for this atomic

pyrit_scorer.description: PyRIT scorer class name to use for success evaluation

payload.description: Optional explicit payload file reference (relative to the atomic's directory). When omitted, the runner falls back to parsing the body for `payloads/...` references and to a single .json/.md file in payloads/.

success_indicators.description: Optional list of substrings the runner's scorer matches (case-insensitive, any-of) against the agent's response to declare success. Replaces the heuristic of using the first line of '## Success criteria' prose as the literal substring (which never matches realistic responses). When omitted, the runner falls back to the legacy heuristic for backwards compatibility.

seed_prompt.description: Optional concrete attack-string sent verbatim as the objective when no attacker LLM is available. With an attacker LLM the seed serves as an initial mutation seed; without one (e.g., ATOMIC_ATLAS_NO_ATTACKER_LLM=1 or no API key) it IS the attack string. Falls back to the atomic's '## Interaction' body section if not set.

judge_guidance.description: Optional prose hint prepended to the LLM judge's true_description. Promptfoo's graderGuidance equivalent — biases the judge toward the technique-specific signal without rewriting success criteria.

verdict.description: Expected judge verdict for this response.

reason.description: Optional one-line explanation of why this response gets that verdict.

judge_examples.description: Optional list of {response, verdict, reason} triples spliced into the judge's metadata. Promptfoo's graderExamples equivalent — concrete pass/fail examples improve judge accuracy.

strategy.description: Override scorer-tier selection. 'auto' lets the runner pick (judge if API key present, else indicators, else substring). 'composite' requires the 'scorers' field.

aggregator.description: How to combine child scorer verdicts in composite strategy.

refusal_check.description: Refusal short-circuit mode. 'cheap' = substring of curated refusal phrases (no LLM call). 'llm' = pyrit.score.SelfAskRefusalScorer (one extra LLM call). 'off' disables.

judge_model.description: Per-atomic judge model override (e.g., 'gpt-4o-mini'). Falls back to ATOMIC_ATLAS_ATTACKER_MODEL env or PyRIT default.

name.description: Key under which matches are stored in evidence.extracted.

pattern.description: Python regex applied to the agent's response. All matches are collected into evidence.extracted[name].

extractors.description: Optional regex-based extractors for capturing artifacts (credentials, file fragments, system-prompt leaks) from the response. Pure regex — no LLM. Matches accumulate into evidence.extracted.
